title: अन्नपूर्णा स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री अन्नपूर्णा स्तोत्रम् ॥

नित्यानन्दकरी वराभयकरी सौन्दर्यरत्नाकरी
निर्धूताखिलघोरपावनकरी प्रत्यक्षमाहेश्वरी।
प्रालेयाचलवंशपावनकरी काशीपुराधीश्वरी
भिक्षां देहि कृपावलम्बनकरी माताऽन्नपूर्णेश्वरी॥१॥

नानारत्नविचित्रभूषणकरी हेमाम्बराडम्बरी
मुक्ताहारविलम्बमानविलसद्वक्षोजकुम्भान्तरी।
काश्मीरागरुवासिताङ्गरुचिरे काशीपुराधीश्वरी
भिक्षां देहि कृपावलम्बनकरी माताऽन्नपूर्णेश्वरी॥२॥

योगानन्दकरी रिपुक्षयकरी धर्मैकनिष्ठाकरी
चन्द्रार्कानलभासमानलहरी त्रैलोक्यरक्षाकरी।
सर्वैश्वर्यसमस्तवाञ्छितकरी काशीपुराधीश्वरी
भिक्षां देहि कृपावलम्बनकरी माताऽन्नपूर्णेश्वरी॥३॥

कैलासाचलकन्दरालयकरी गौरी ह्युमा शांकरी
कौमारी निगमार्थगोचरकरी ह्योंकारबीजाक्षरी।
मोक्षद्वारकवाटपाटनकरी काशीपुराधीश्वरी
भिक्षां देहि कृपावलम्बनकरी माताऽन्नपूर्णेश्वरी॥४॥

दृश्यादृश्यविभूतिवाहनकरी ब्रह्माण्डभाण्डोदरी
लीलानाटकसूत्रभेदनकरी विज्ञानदीपांकुरी।
श्रीविश्वेशमनःप्रसादनकरी काशीपुराधीश्वरी
भिक्षां देहि कृपावलम्बनकरी माताऽन्नपूर्णेश्वरी॥५॥

उर्वीसर्वजनेश्वरी भगवती माताऽन्नपूर्णेश्वरी
वेणीनीलसमानकुन्तलधरी नित्यान्नदानेश्वरी।
सर्वानन्दकरी सदाशुभकरी काशीपुराधीश्वरी
भिक्षां देहि कृपावलम्बनकरी माताऽन्नपूर्णेश्वरी॥६॥

आदिक्षान्तसमस्तवर्णनकरी शम्भोस्त्रिभावाकरी
काश्मीरात्रिजलेश्वरी त्रिलहरी नित्यांकुरा शर्वरी।
कामाकांक्षकरी जनोदयकरी काशीपुराधीश्वरी
भिक्षां देहि कृपावलम्बनकरी माताऽन्नपूर्णेश्वरी॥७॥

देवी सर्वविचित्ररत्नरचिता दाक्षायणी सुन्दरी
वामे स्वादुपयोधरा प्रियकरी सौभाग्यमाहेश्वरी।
भक्ताभीष्टकरी सदाशुभकरी काशीपुराधीश्वरी
भिक्षां देहि कृपावलम्बनकरी माताऽन्नपूर्णेश्वरी॥८॥

चन्द्रार्कानलकोटिकोटिसदृशा तन्वी तपस्वी यशा
लक्ष्मीर्वाणि रमा ह्युमा ह्यपरणा राजत्कृपापाशिनी।
सौभाग्यैश्वर्यकामदा प्रियकरी काशीपुराधीश्वरी
भिक्षां देहि कृपावलम्बनकरी माताऽन्नपूर्णेश्वरी॥९॥

क्षत्रत्राणकरी महाऽभयकरी माता कृपासागरी
साक्षान्मोक्षकरी सदा शिवकरी विश्वेश्वरी श्रीधरी।
दक्षाक्रन्दकरी निरामयकरी काशीपुराधीश्वरी
भिक्षां देहि कृपावलम्बनकरी माताऽन्नपूर्णेश्वरी॥१०॥

अन्नपूर्णे सदापूर्णे शंकरप्राणवल्लभे।
ज्ञानवैराग्यसिद्ध्यर्थं भिक्षां देहि च पार्वति॥११॥

माता च पार्वती देवी पिता देवो महेश्वरः।
बान्धवाः शिवभक्ताश्च स्वदेशो भुवनत्रयम्॥१२॥

॥ इति श्रीमच्छंकराचार्यविरचितम् अन्नपूर्णा स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Annapurna Stotram ||

Nityananda-kari Varabhaya-kari Saundarya-ratnakari
Nirdhuta-akhila-ghora-pavanakari Pratyaksha-maheshwari.
Praleya-achala-vamsha-pavanakari Kashipura-adhishwari
Bhiksham Dehi Kripa-avalambana-kari Mata Annapurneshwari.||1||

Nana-ratna-vichitra-bhushanakari Hemambara-adambari
Mukta-hara-vilambamana-vilasad-vaksho-ja-kumbhantari.
Kashmira-agaru-vasita-anga-ruchire Kashipura-adhishwari
Bhiksham Dehi Kripa-avalambana-kari Mata Annapurneshwari.||2||

Yogananda-kari Ripu-kshaya-kari Dharmaika-nishtha-kari
Chandra-arka-anala-bhasamana-lahari Trailokya-raksha-kari.
Sarvaishvarya-samasta-vanchhita-kari Kashipura-adhishwari
Bhiksham Dehi Kripa-avalambana-kari Mata Annapurneshwari.||3||

Kailasa-achala-kandaralaya-kari Gauri Hyuma Shankari
Kaumari Nigamartha-gochara-kari Hyomkara-bijakshhari.
Moksha-dvara-kavata-patana-kari Kashipura-adhishwari
Bhiksham Dehi Kripa-avalambana-kari Mata Annapurneshwari.||4||

Drishya-adrishya-vibhuti-vahana-kari Brahmanda-bhandodari
Lila-nataka-sutra-bhedana-kari Vijnana-dipankuri.
Shri Vishwesha-manah-prasadana-kari Kashipura-adhishwari
Bhiksham Dehi Kripa-avalambana-kari Mata Annapurneshwari.||5||

Urvi-sarva-janeshwari Bhagavati Mata Annapurneshwari
Veni-nila-samana-kuntala-dhari Nitya-anna-daneshwari.
Sarvananda-kari Sadashubha-kari Kashipura-adhishwari
Bhiksham Dehi Kripa-avalambana-kari Mata Annapurneshwari.||6||

Adi-kshanta-samasta-varnana-kari Shambhos-tribhavakari
Kashmira-atri-jaleshwari Tri-lahari Nityankura Sharvari.
Kama-akanksha-kari Janodaya-kari Kashipura-adhishwari
Bhiksham Dehi Kripa-avalambana-kari Mata Annapurneshwari.||7||

Devi Sarva-vichitra-ratna-rachita Dakshayani Sundari
Vame Svadu-payodhara Priya-kari Saubhagya-maheshwari.
Bhakta-abhishta-kari Sada-shubha-kari Kashipura-adhishwari
Bhiksham Dehi Kripa-avalambana-kari Mata Annapurneshwari.||8||

Chandra-arka-anala-koti-koti-sadrisha Tanvi Tapaswi Yasha
Lakshmir Vani Rama Hyuma Hyaparana Rajat-kripa-pashini.
Saubhagya-aishvarya-kamada Priya-kari Kashipura-adhishwari
Bhiksham Dehi Kripa-avalambana-kari Mata Annapurneshwari.||9||

Kshatra-trana-kari Maha-abhaya-kari Mata Kripa-sagari
Sakshat Moksha-kari Sada Shiva-kari Vishveshwari Shri-dhari.
Daksha-akranda-kari Niramaya-kari Kashipura-adhishwari
Bhiksham Dehi Kripa-avalambana-kari Mata Annapurneshwari.||10||

Annapurne Sadapurne Shankara-prana-vallabhe.
Jnana-vairagya-siddhyartham Bhiksham Dehi Cha Parvati.||11||

Mata Cha Parvati Devi Pita Devo Maheshwarah.
Bandhavah Shiva-bhaktash Cha Svadesho Bhuvana-trayam.||12||

|| Iti Shrimach-Shankaracharya-virachitam Annapurna Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
हे नित्य आनन्द देने वाली! हे वर और अभय देने वाली! हे सौन्दर्य की रत्नाकर! हे समस्त घोर पापों को पवित्र करने वाली! हे साक्षात् माहेश्वरी! हे हिमालय वंश को पवित्र करने वाली! हे काशीपुर की अधीश्वरी! हे कृपा का अवलम्बन देने वाली माता अन्नपूर्णेश्वरी! मुझे भिक्षा दो।

हे कैलास की कन्दराओं में निवास करने वाली! हे गौरी! हे उमा! हे शांकरी! हे मोक्ष के द्वार के कपाट खोलने वाली! भिक्षा दो।

हे अन्नपूर्णे! सदा पूर्ण! हे शंकर की प्राणवल्लभे! ज्ञान और वैराग्य की सिद्धि के लिए मुझे भिक्षा दो, हे पार्वती!

माता पार्वती देवी हैं, पिता महेश्वर हैं, बान्धव शिवभक्त हैं और तीनों लोक अपना देश है।
benefits: Invokes Goddess Annapurna for abundance of food and nourishment
Invokes freedom from hunger, poverty, and material deprivation
Invokes the blessing of spiritual knowledge (jnana) and dispassion (vairagya)
Especially powerful when recited in Kashi (Varanasi)
One of Shankaracharya's most celebrated devotional compositions
description: The Annapurna Stotram, known in Sanskrit as अन्नपूर्णा स्तोत्रम्, is a devotional hymn of praise in praise of Goddess Parvati. Attributed to Composed by Adi Shankaracharya, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to open the doors to moksha (liberation) and bestow spiritual knowledge (jnana) and dispassion (vairagya). It is also said to grant freedom from hunger, poverty, and material deprivation. This sacred text is ideally recited on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Navratri and Annapurna Jayanti for enhanced blessings. At 12 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 8 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Annapurna Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Annapurna Stotram by Shankaracharya in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Sacred hymn to Goddess Annapurna for food, abundance and spiritual wisdom.

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Annapurna Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Annapurna Stotram, also called the Annapurna Ashtakam, was composed by Adi Shankaracharya (788-820 CE), the great Advaita Vedanta acharya, during his pilgrimage to Kashi (Varanasi) where Annapurna is the principal goddess presiding over the holy city alongside her consort Vishvanatha. Tradition records that Shankaracharya composed the hymn in the presence of the Annapurna Devi murti at Kashi, praising her as the universal mother who feeds all beings. The hymn comprises eight verses praising Annapurna's beauty, generosity and cosmic role, plus the famous concluding shloka Mata cha Parvati Devi Pita Devo Maheshvarah — Mother is Goddess Parvati, Father is Lord Shiva — which has become one of the most recited prayers in Hindu households across India. (Sources: Wikipedia Adi Shankara; Hindupedia Annapurna; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Goddess Annapurna and what does the stotram praise?
seoFaqs.answer: Annapurna — meaning the giver of food (anna) in fullness (purna) — is the form of Goddess Parvati who, according to puranic narrative, descended to Kashi to demonstrate that food is divine and not mere matter. The story holds that Lord Shiva once declared the material world to be illusion (maya); offended that food itself was thus dismissed, Parvati withdrew, and famine struck the three worlds. The gods, sages and even Shiva himself were forced to beg for food, and Parvati appeared in Kashi as Annapurna, distributing food from her golden ladle. Shiva himself stood as her first beggar, and from that day Annapurna has been worshipped as the cosmic mother who sustains all beings through food. The stotram opens with the famous refrain Bhiksham Dehi Krpavalambana-kari — please give alms, granter of compassionate support. (Sources: Hindupedia Annapurna; Wikipedia Kashi Annapurna; Skanda Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the famous concluding shloka of the Annapurna Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Annapurna Stotram concludes with one of the most beloved and universally recited shlokas in Hindu tradition: Mata cha Parvati Devi Pita Devo Maheshvarah, Bandhavaah Shiva-bhaktaa-sca, svadeshah bhuvana-trayam — translated as Mother is Goddess Parvati, Father is the Lord Maheshvara, Kinsmen are the devotees of Shiva, and the Three Worlds are my homeland. This shloka is recited by countless devotees across India before meals, before journeys, at the start of any auspicious activity, and at the conclusion of evening sandhya. It expresses the supreme Vedantic insight that all beings are family in the cosmic sense, with Shiva and Parvati as the universal parents of all creation. The shloka often appears on temple walls and in household puja rooms across India. (Sources: Annapurna Stotram; Stotra Nidhi; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the best time to recite the Annapurna Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Daily recitation before the first meal of the day is the most beloved practice — many devotees consider it inauspicious to begin eating without first reciting at least the concluding Mata cha Parvati shloka. The supreme annual occasion is Annapurna Jayanti, falling on Margashirsha Purnima (December), when devotees observe a fast, perform Annapurna puja, and recite the full ashtakam 1, 11, 21 or 108 times. Akshaya Tritiya in Vaishakha is another major occasion — the day on which Annapurna's golden patra is said to grant inexhaustible food. Other recommended occasions include Friday (the Devi-day), Annakuta on the day after Diwali (when mountains of food are offered to Krishna and Annapurna), and during pilgrimages to Kashi Vishvanath and Annapurna temple. (Sources: Drikpanchang Annapurna Jayanti; Hindupedia; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: Where is the principal Annapurna temple?
seoFaqs.answer: The principal Annapurna temple is the Annapurna Devi Mandir in Kashi (Varanasi), Uttar Pradesh — one of the most sacred Devi shrines in India, located near the Kashi Vishvanath Jyotirlinga in the heart of the holy city. The temple was rebuilt in the 18th century by Maharani Ahilyabai Holkar of Indore. Tradition holds that the Annapurna of Kashi is the original cosmic Annapurna who sustains all beings, and that the food (prasad) distributed at her temple never runs out. Other significant Annapurna temples include the Annapurneshwari Temple at Horanadu in Karnataka's Western Ghats, the Annapurna shrine at the Madurai Meenakshi temple, and many regional Annapurna shrines across Maharashtra, Bengal and Tamil Nadu. (Sources: Wikipedia Annapurna Devi Mandir; Hindupedia Kashi; Drikpanchang.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Annapurna Stotram promise?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional sources promise that one who recites the Annapurna Stotram daily with bhakti is never deprived of food or basic sustenance throughout life — Annapurna's grace ensures that the household always has nourishment, however modest. Specific benefits include prosperity in agriculture and business, abundance in the kitchen and granary, the guest never being turned away hungry, recovery from eating disorders and digestive disease, fertility and the welfare of children, and harmony in family meals. Spiritual rewards include devotion to Parvati and Shiva, recognition of food as a divine sacrament, and the deepening of compassion toward the hungry — many devotees who recite the stotram daily are inspired to feed the poor (anna-dana) as the supreme charity. (Sources: Hindupedia Annapurna; Stotra Nidhi; Drikpanchang.)

seoFaqs.question: Can the Annapurna Stotram be recited by anyone?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes. As a Shankara composition openly preserved across all Hindu sampradayas, the Annapurna Stotram is accessible to all devotees regardless of gender, caste or age, and no formal diksha is required for daily recitation. Children across India are commonly taught at least the concluding Mata cha Parvati shloka as their first prayer, often recited together as a family before meals. Women include the full ashtakam in the daily morning puja for the welfare and abundance of the kitchen and family. The only customary requirements are cleanliness of body and place, sitting facing east or north, and ideally offering a small portion of cooked food (naivedya) at the household altar before consumption. The recitation makes every meal a sacred sacrament. (Sources: Hindupedia; Stotra Nidhi; Sanatan Sanstha.)
